gate_claim: the guard-hardening repair makes the discard-archive commit impossible to bind to a non-surviving branch: the base-branch guard rejects the detached-HEAD sentinel 'HEAD' and every falsified base_branch (the discarded feature branch, an arbitrary current lane) at BOTH call sites, and the post-commit verification re-resolves the checkout and proves reachability from base so a concurrent HEAD re-point downgrades to a truthful discard_archive_committed false with residue recoverable instead of a false committed true — with both F1 triggers, commit success, diff-quiet race, commit failure, offline, the honest-state postures, the full preflight classification matrix, four-edition parity, and the documentation delta all still holding
gate_surface: the complete epoch-3 candidate: the claim.js four-edition family (canonical, codex byte twin, gitlab and gitea hand ports), the claude-chain test surfaces (simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js, test-claim-hardening.js), and the documentation delta, reviewed against the epoch-2 n5 refutation evidence and the accumulated diff vs the claim root base

verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
review_summary: no_blocking_findings
review_attestation: full_review_completed

## Candidate and context

- Candidate = accumulated worktree diff vs HEAD in .kw/worktrees/issue-715 (verified: git rev-parse HEAD == claim_root_base cd28e8e52fb641cf2173ced57c91a042e3c13e1e, matching the review context). Exactly 14 modified files — the claim.js/sink-merge.js four-edition family, scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js, scripts/test-claim-hardening.js, scripts/test-sink-merge.js, CHANGELOG.md, docs/api.md, docs/workflow-state-contract.md — plus the untracked kaola-workflow/issue-715/ run tree: zero out-of-set writes.
- Epoch-3 delta read in full: commitDiscardArchive hardened INSIDE the helper (scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js:2364-2469) — N5-A clauses (a) sentinel rejection (:2389-2393), (b) argument-array rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<base> (:2394-2406), (c) discardedBranch refusal (:2407-2413), (d) sweep defaultBase constraint (:2414-2422), the retained epoch-2 string-equality guard (:2423-2427); N5-B post-commit re-resolution (:2444-2453) + merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base reachability (:2454-2461) ahead of the epoch-1 cat-file presence check (:2462-2464), all inside the existing never-throw try (:2466-2468). Call sites: release hoist releaseBaseBranch (:3422) + call with { discardedBranch: featureBranch || null } (:3464-3465) + loud warnings[] entry on failure (:3490-3492) + emit (:3496-3499); sweep pre-read of base_branch BEFORE the archive move + default fallback (:4430-4438) + call with { discardedBranch: folder.branch || null, defaultBase: sweepDefaultBase } (:4452-4453) + cleanup-entry emit (:4492-4495). Exports (:4790-4793).
- Review context honored structurally: upstream n2-guard-hardening-docs read in full (nonce 29e34dc5f164 matches its line-1 evidence-binding); the epoch-2 n5 refutation (n5-falsify-branch-commit 7e6482a523a9) adjudicated component by component from kaola-workflow/issue-715/.cache/epochs/2/files/.cache/n5-falsify-branch-commit.md; n1 RED/GREEN evidence (f6077183028a) and my epoch-2 predecessor certification (45da7230e295, lineage only) read in full.
- Role honored read-only: no repository file created, modified, or deleted. All verification was Read/Grep/diff plus the shipped suites (which fixture under os.tmpdir() and clean up) plus my own probes built under $TMPDIR/kw715-n3/ driving the REAL shipped helper via require() against throwaway tmp repos.

## N5-A adjudication — RESOLVED (all four falsified-base cells now refuse BEFORE staging, at BOTH call sites)

The epoch-2 guard was pure string equality against an unvalidated operator-controlled base. The epoch-3 helper keeps that equality as the final gate but rejects every non-surviving base class before any staging, and each clause is proven by an executed end-to-end test plus my own probes:

- B4a (release, detached HEAD, base_branch falsified to 'HEAD'): clause (a) rejects the sentinel outright (:2389) — it is by definition not a branch. Executed GREEN: testReleaseDetachedHeadLyingBaseSkipsArchiveCommit (standalone AND in the full suite) asserts exit 0 released:true, discard_archive_committed === false, discard_archive_branch === 'HEAD', detached tip AND main tip byte-unchanged, no chore commit on any ref (gitLogAllSubjects), residue on disk AND manually committable on main afterwards. Unit pin in test-claim-hardening.js asserts the same refusal with tip unchanged + residue.
- W5 (watch-pr CLOSED sweep, detached, base 'HEAD'): identical refusal via the second call site — executed GREEN (testWatchPrClosedSweepDetachedLyingBaseHeadSkips): cleanup entry committed:false + branch 'HEAD', both tips unchanged, no chore commit, residue.
- B3 (release on feature branch, base falsified to the discarded branch itself): clause (c) refuses base === opts.discardedBranch (:2409) — the release KNOWS the branch it discards and passes it (:3465). Executed GREEN: testReleaseOnFeatureBranchLyingBaseNamesDiscardedBranchSkips asserts committed:false, branch disclosed, detail naming the discarded branch, BOTH ref tips unchanged, the archive a tree at NEITHER ref, residue recoverable.
- W6 (sweep on workflow/other-lane, base falsified to that lane): only clause (d) catches this cell — the lane is a real ref (passes b), is not the folder's own discarded lane (passes c), and equals the current checkout (passes epoch-2 equality). (d) refuses any non-default base at the sweep posture (:2417). Executed GREEN: testWatchPrClosedSweepArbitraryLaneLyingBaseSkips asserts committed:false + branch 'workflow/other-lane', both tips unchanged, no chore commit, residue; the (d-sweep) hardening pin asserts the refusal happens BEFORE staging (tip unchanged).
- My own additional probes against the real helper (not shipped, built under $TMPDIR): a whitespace-padded ' HEAD ' is trimmed and rejected as the sentinel; a revsyntax base 'main^{commit}' passes clause (b)'s verify but is refused by the epoch-2 equality guard (rev-parse --abbrev-ref never prints revsyntax); a tag named as base fails the refs/heads/ verify; the honest on-base path still commits (tree at main); every refusal left the feature tip byte-unchanged (pre-staging). The precondition honestly stated by n5 holds: both claim paths clamp baseBranch to '' for 'HEAD'/self (claim.js:1201 and :1405), so the tooling never writes the falsified values — a hand-edit or external corruption remains required, and within that precondition every demonstrated cell is now refused.

## N5-B adjudication — RESOLVED (the check-then-act window now downgrades truthfully)

The epoch-2 helper verified only presence at the CURRENT HEAD. The epoch-3 helper re-resolves the checkout after the commit (:2446 rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) and requires it to still equal the guarded base, then requires the HEAD commit to be reachable from base (:2455 merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD base, fail-closed on any probe error), before the retained cat-file presence check.

- RC1 (deterministic git shim interposing at add -A -- <rel>: real add, then symbolic-ref HEAD onto race + checkout): executed GREEN by testReleaseHeadRepointRaceDowngradesArchiveCommit — the emit DOWNGRADES to discard_archive_committed === false with discard_archive_branch === 'race' (the ACTUAL receiver), the assertion !JSON.includes('"discard_archive_branch":"main"') pins never-emit-the-stale-base, main tip byte-unchanged, the archive NOT a tree at main, recoverable as a tree at race with the chore commit on race's tip. The direct helper pin in test-claim-hardening.js asserts the same downgrade + actual-receiver disclosure + base-tip-unchanged + recoverability.
- Reachability depth: the merge-base check adds the reset/remove class a branch-name recheck alone cannot catch (base rewound so the commit is no longer an ancestor), and it fails closed.
- My own stronger probe (two-interposition adversary: shim switches HEAD to race at add AND back to main at commit — the commit lands on race while the re-resolution reads main): the helper NEVER emitted committed:true — it failed closed through the cat-file presence check to { committed: false, branch: 'main', detail: 'discard archive commit failed: ...' } while the commit was verified a tree at race. The durability claim is never falsified; the residual is a conservative failure-path disclosure under an adversary strictly stronger than RC1 (recorded as O-N3-1 below, non-blocking).
- RC2 preserved (diff-quiet race): when the concurrent process commits the staged tree ON base between add and commit, the helper skips its own commit (no duplicate), the re-resolution passes (still base), reachability passes (HEAD == base tip), cat-file sees the tree — committed:true + branch base, truthful. The epoch-2 RC2 pin is inside the re-run 485-assertion suite.

## Design decision (d) — ruled SOUND

n1's self-declared decision, quoted verbatim for adjudication: "(d) at the sweep posture base must equal opts.defaultBase (the sweep has only the pre-read operator-controlled state base and no restore step, so only the repo's default branch is provably surviving-and-integration — a falsified base naming the current arbitrary lane is refused; the release's restored base carries no default constraint because the restore itself established it)."

- Necessity (proof from the refutation): W6 demonstrated that a falsified base naming the current arbitrary lane commits onto that lane and orphans on natural lane deletion. No other clause closes W6 — the lane is a real local ref (b passes), it is not the folder's own discarded lane (c passes), and it equals the current checkout (the epoch-2 equality passes). The post-commit N5-B checks cannot catch it either (branchAfter === base; HEAD trivially an ancestor of itself). Only a default-branch constraint at the sweep refuses it. Executed proof: the W6 walkthrough test and the (d-sweep) pin both green.
- Soundness of the asymmetry (proof from code): the release needs no default constraint because its restore PHYSICALLY establishes the base — git checkout releaseBaseBranch succeeds only for a real ref, the release then deletes only the feature branch, and clause (c) still refuses the one branch the release provably kills; a falsified nonexistent base fails the restore's checkout and then clause (b); a falsified base naming another live lane becomes the operator's actual restored checkout — the recorded base has always been the authoritative restore target (pre-existing semantics, :3440-3447) — with the disclosure accurate. The sweep has no such step: its only base evidence is the operator-controlled durable string, so the repo default (defaultBranch()'s offline-safe probe chain, :575-601) is the only provably surviving-and-integration branch there.
- Not over-strict in any harmful direction: the constraint fails CLOSED. For a repo whose integration branch differs from the resolved default, a sweep skip is loud (committed:false + branch + detail on the cleanup entry), leaves recoverable residue, and the documented manual-commit path applies — never a silent loss, never a false true. The supported honest posture is proven green by the hardening pin ({ discardedBranch, defaultBase: 'main' } on main commits, tree at main, merge-base ancestor status 0) and by the W1 epoch-1 sweep-success cell in the re-run walkthrough. Honest tooling-written bases are the operator's claim-time checkout (:1201, :1405), so the constraint binds only on falsified state or a genuinely non-default integration lane, and there only into a truthful, recoverable skip.
- Not under-strict: combined with (a)-(c) and the epoch-2 equality, every falsified-base class demonstrated in epoch-2 (sentinel, discarded branch, arbitrary lane, nonexistent ref, ghost/whitespace/revsyntax/tag variants I probed) is refused before staging at the posture where it can occur.

## Truthful-reporting matrix (every path verified against code + executed tests)

- Success: committed:true + branch:base at both emit sites (:3496-3498, :4492-4494) — R0/R1/W1 cells green in the re-run suites; my P4 probe committed with tree at main.
- Diff-quiet skip (RC2): committed:true + branch:base, no duplicate chore commit — green.
- Commit failure (R2a hook failure / R2b ignored add): exit 0 released:true (never stranded), committed:false + branch + detail + loud warnings[] entry — green.
- Non-base skip (honest B1/W4 postures): committed:false + branch disclosed + 'not the surviving base branch' detail, residue recoverable — green.
- Guard refusal (sentinel/falsified base): committed:false + branch disclosed + clause-specific detail + warnings[] (release) / cleanups[] detail (sweep); zero commits anywhere (tip-unchanged + no-chore-commit assertions in all five new tests) — green.
- Race downgrade (RC1): committed:false with the ACTUAL receiving branch disclosed, never the stale pre-race base (explicit never-main assertion); base tip unchanged; off-base commit recoverable — green.
- The receiving (or non-receiving) branch is disclosed on EVERY outcome — discard_archive_branch is emitted unconditionally at both sites; discard_archive_commit_detail only on failure. Offline: the helper body contains zero OFFLINE branches (grep count 0) — a commit that runs is never skipped for offline; every release walkthrough fixture ran KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1.

## Epoch-2 preservation (all surviving behaviors untouched and green)

- Both F1 triggers: testReleaseInPlaceOnFeatureBranchCommitsArchiveOnBase and testWatchPrClosedSweepSkipsCommitOffBaseBranch each pass standalone (--only) AND inside the full walkthrough — Trigger A commits on the restored base with reachability proven, Trigger B truthfully skips off-base with both tips unchanged.
- treeDirty third parameter + segment-boundary exemption + conditional -uall (:537-573) byte-identical to the epoch-2 repair (the epoch-3 diff adds nothing there); the two other treeDirty callers pass no exemption.
- Receipt/dest exemption matrix: SINK_RECEIPT_EXEMPT in sink-merge.js is the byte-identical epoch-1 regex (the sink-merge.js delta vs HEAD is exactly the epoch-1 18 +/- lines, no epoch-3 hunks); test-sink-merge.js passed 105 assertions including the #715 (l) over-exemption guard and (m) own-receipt regression lock.
- Honest-state postures (B1/W4 detached, B2 symbolic-ref resolution, B4b lying 'HEAD' on the feature branch), the honest on-base commit, commit-failure paths, and the never-parked baseline pin all green inside the 485-assertion claim-hardening run (464 epoch-2 + 21 new = 485, arithmetic consistent with n1's report).

## Four-edition parity (proven, not inferred)

- Codex twin: diff scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js EMPTY; same for sink-merge.js — byte-identical.
- Helper body md5 c767f927efd3f6c507ae159194b4bcf2 re-computed by this reviewer over function commitDiscardArchive...} in canonical, gitlab, and gitea — identical in all three, exactly as n1 reported. Exactly 3 commitDiscardArchive( occurrences per file (1 definition + 2 call sites) in all four trees — no missed or extra site.
- Call-site hunks mirrored in both forge ports: releaseBaseBranch hoist + restore usage + call with discardedBranch only; sweepBaseBranch pre-read + default fallback + sweepDefaultBase + call with both opts; emit disclosures (committed/branch always, detail on failure); exports — all present (gitlab :3140/:3182-3183/:3212-3215/:3590-3613/:3652-3655/:4490; gitea :3135/:3177-3178/:3207-3210/:3585-3608/:3647-3650/:4482).
- node scripts/validate-script-sync.js -> OK (25 common scripts, 28 byte-identical groups, 8 rename-normalized families, 7 forge export-superset families in sync); node scripts/edition-sync.js --check -> parity (12 forge aggregator ports, 25 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, 28 byte-identical groups).

## RED-first reproduction and test quality

- RED is real and candidate-caused: n1's recorded pre-impl failures name all five inverted cells with the exact epoch-2 refutation outcomes (committed:true on 'HEAD' / the discarded branch / the arbitrary lane / the stale 'main'), and the six claim-hardening pins failed pre-impl with matching strings. The shipped assertion strings match the recorded RED output verbatim.
- GREEN is executed, not inferred, and was re-executed by THIS reviewer in the worktree: node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js -> "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed" (full suite), and each of the five new tests plus the two epoch-2 F1 tests passed standalone via --only; node scripts/test-claim-hardening.js -> "claim-hardening tests passed (485 assertions)"; node scripts/test-sink-merge.js -> 105 assertions; node scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js -> all 149 passed; node scripts/test-kimi-edition.js -> 577 assertions; node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js -> 547 assertions; node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js and node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js -> both passed. All exit 0.
- Test quality: the five walkthrough tests drive the REAL claim.js end-to-end via spawnSync against tmp fixtures (deterministic git shims for the race, gh shims for the sweep), assert ref tips byte-unchanged, no chore commit on ANY ref (git log --all), archive presence/absence at specific refs via cat-file, residue recoverability including a manual commit on base, and the never-stale-base JSON pin. The hardening pins exercise the exported helper directly with tip-unchanged proof that refusals precede staging. No vacuous assertions found.

## Documentation delta (matches the shipped fields and semantics exactly)

- CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] ### Fixed: the guard-hardening entry (appended directly after the epoch-2 #715 entry; prior entries untouched) states sentinel rejection, argument-array rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<base>, the discarded-branch refusal with call-site-supplied branch, the sweep defaultBase constraint with the exact sound rationale, the post-commit re-resolution + merge-base --is-ancestor re-verification, and the truthful downgrade disclosing the ACTUAL receiving branch — every clause verified against helper :2389-2461.
- docs/api.md: the NATIVE=0 in-place paragraph AND the Closure Contract "Discard-archive commit (issue #715)" paragraph name exactly the shipped fields — discard_archive_committed (truthfully false on an off-base skip, a refused base, or a failed re-verification), discard_archive_branch on BOTH success and skip, discard_archive_commit_detail + warnings[] on failure — matching the emit sites :3496-3499 and :4492-4495 and the helper result shape { committed, branch, detail }. n2's cited anchors (helper 2364-2469, emits 3496-3499/4492-4495) are byte-accurate.
- docs/workflow-state-contract.md § Terminal journal disposal: the added validation-and-truthful-downgrade sentences match the code (sentinel/falsified-base refusal before staging; post-commit re-verification; downgrade disclosing the actual receiver; residue recoverable). No doc sentence claims behavior absent from the helper; no field names invented.

## Scope, obligations, and non-blocking observations

- Zero out-of-set writes; the candidate is exactly the declared 14-file family. Both n5 findings adjudicated resolved; every gate_claim clause verified against the diff and by executed tests; both upstream readings (n1, n2) cross-checked against the shipped code.
- Epoch-2 non-blocking observations stand as classified: O-N5-1 (the pre-existing silent removeBranch swallow in the restore else-branch) and O-N5-2 / parent O1 (the degenerate reserved-name receipt forms the exemption regex matches — classification-only) bind to surfaces this candidate deliberately did not touch; not re-admitted.
- O-N3-1 (new, non-blocking): under a two-interposition adversary strictly stronger than RC1 (HEAD re-pointed away for the commit AND back before the re-resolution), the helper fails closed via the cat-file presence check to committed:false with the raw git error in detail — never a false committed:true (probe-executed by this reviewer). The failure-path branch disclosure then names the current branch while the commit is recoverable on the actual receiver; the durability claim itself can never be falsified this way. Conservative direction, bounded to a sub-millisecond double-switch adversary; classification-only.

findings_none: true


domain_outcome: approved

review_conclusion: the epoch-3 guard-hardening closes both epoch-2 refutations by construction and by executed test — the in-helper guard now rejects the 'HEAD' sentinel and every falsified non-surviving base (discarded branch, nonexistent ref, arbitrary lane) BEFORE staging at both call sites, so the B4a/W5/B3/W6 cells can no longer commit anywhere; the post-commit re-resolution plus merge-base reachability check downgrades the RC1 raced commit to a truthful discard_archive_committed:false with the ACTUAL receiving branch disclosed and never the stale base; the sweep defaultBase decision is ruled sound (necessary for W6, justified by the restore asymmetry, fail-closed in cost); every epoch-2 surviving behavior — both F1 triggers, the receipt/dest exemption matrix, the honest-state postures, diff-quiet, commit-failure, offline — re-ran green; four-edition parity is proven (codex byte twin, helper md5 c767f927efd3f6c507ae159194b4bcf2 in all three, validate-script-sync OK, edition-sync parity, forge ports mirror every hunk); the docs match the shipped field names and semantics; and my own independent probes (whitespace sentinel, revsyntax and tag bases, the stronger double-switch race) found no counterexample — zero admitted findings.
